revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iya-jinja]][[ja:揖夜神社]] {{Expand Japanese|揖夜神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iya Shrine | native_name = 揖夜神社 | image = Izumo-iya-jinja-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iya Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.430532|133.154087}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Izanami|lt=Izanami|ar|إزانامي|az|İzanami|bcl|Izanami|bs|Izanami|ca|Izanami|da|Izanami|el|Ιζανάμι|en|Izanami|eo|Izanami|es|Izanami|fa|ایزانامی|fi|Izanami|fr|Izanami|he|איזאנמי|hr|Izanami|hu|Izanami|hy|Իզանամի|id|Izanami|it|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|ko|이자나미|mk|Изанами|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯃꯤ|pt|Izanami|ru|Идзанами|simple|Izanami|sl|Izanami|sr|Изанами|sv|Izanami|ta|இசநமி|th|อิซานามิ|tl|Izanami|tr|Izanami|uk|Ідзанамі|zh|伊邪那美|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iya Shrine'''|揖夜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q207321|ltq=Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}} * {{ill|Izumo Province|lt=Izumo Province|ar|إزومو (مقاطعة)|ca|Província d'Izumo|cs|Provincie Izumo|de|Provinz Izumo|en|Izumo Province|es|Provincia de Izumo|fa|ولایت ایزومو|fr|Province d'Izumo|gl|Provincia de Izumo|id|Provinsi Izumo|it|Izumo (provincia)|ja|出雲国|ka|იზუმოს პროვინცია|ko|이즈모국|lt|Idzumo žemė|nl|Izumo (provincie)|no|Izumo|pt|Província de Izumo|ru|Идзумо (провинция)|simple|Izumo Province|sk|Izumo (provincia)|sr|Провинција Изумо|tr|Izumo (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ідзумо|wuu|出云国|zh_classical|出雲國|zh_min_nan|Izumo-kok|zh_yue|出雲國|zh|出雲國|qid=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}} * {{ill|Q11073594|ltq=Q11073594|qid=Q11073594|1=Ou district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.430532|133.154087}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Izumo-iya-jinja-1.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q682306|ltq=Q682306|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182801 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|ko|정5위|zh|正五位|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いやじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259070615 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7280005000688 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00647598 == Commons category (P373) == * Iya-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Karakuniitateno Shrine|lt=Karakuniitateno Shrine|qid=Q135040786|1=Karakuniitateno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県松江市東出雲町揖屋2229', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県八束郡東出雲町揖屋2229', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iya-jinja}} [[JA:揖夜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11498399}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Iya Shrine |画像 = [[File:Izumo-iya-jinja-1.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}}2229 Iya, Higashiizumo-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|qid=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Hokake Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Iya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Iya Shrine''' (いやじんじゃ / いふやじんじゃ) is a shrine in Iya, Higashiizumo-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Oi six shrines|Q11073594|qid=Q11073594|lt=Oi six shrines|1=Ou district}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly gōsha and later prefectural shrine. It is near a site identified with {{ill|lt=Yomotsu Hirasaka|Q22084535|qid=Q22084535|1=Yomotsu Hirasaka}} from {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|qid=Q814158|lt=Kiki mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. On nearby Hibayama in neighboring Yasugi there is also a related sacred trace identified as Izanami's tomb in the Kojiki. === Enshrined deities === ;Principal deity: {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} ;Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} :{{ill|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} === History === It is identified with "Kotoya-sha" in the {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|qid=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} year-5 entry of the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' ("A dog bit off a dead person's arm and left it at Kotoya-sha"), with "Ifuya-sha" listed as shrine under Jingikan in the Oi District section of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki}},' and with "Iya Shrine" in Oi District, Izumo Province, of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', its {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was raised to Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 (867), then to Senior Fifth Lower in Jōgan 13. According to documents preserved at the shrine, in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}} it was called "Iya Taisha," and from the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}} it was called "Iya Daimyōjin." From the end of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to the Nanboku-chō era, the Ōya clan sent as estate officers served as shrine office called "Bekka" and controlled this shrine. From the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} onward, by order of the {{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11395856|qid=Q11395856|1=Izumo clan}}, the Akigami family priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomusuhi Shrine|Q3192477|qid=Q3192477|1=Kamosu Shrine}} concurrently served as priests. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the Inoue family became Bekka and continue to today. Shizuga, branched from the Inoue family, was a painter using the name Kanaya; his son was master carpenter Kawashima Tokujirō. Even now, construction rites receive offerings from the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko house. === Shrine buildings === ;Haiden ;Honden: {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|qid=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, but interior orientation is opposite that of {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}}. ;Karakuni Itate Shrine: identified with "Ifuya-sha" (Jingikan-listed) in the Izumo Fudoki and Engishiki "Karakuni Itate Shrine seated in same complex." Deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}. Located left of honden. ;Mihotsuhime Shrine: identified with "Ifuya-sha" (not under Jingikan) in the Izumo Fudoki. Deity: {{ill|lt=Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|qid=Q6378698|lt=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. Located right of honden. === Festivals === * Jan 3: Mita-uchi Festival * May 28: Otaue Festival * Aug 28: Hokake Festival * Oct 19: {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} etc. ;Hokake Festival: On the preceding day (27th), priests purify at Sodeshi-ga-ura on {{ill|lt=Nakaumi|Q1077275|qid=Q1077275|1=Nakaumi}}, then prepare offerings such as amazake and roasted rice at the shrine office. On festival day, after morning rites, prepared offerings are presented at 75 locations in the precinct. In the afternoon, the mikoshi is loaded on a boat and carried to Hitotsu-ishi about 500 m offshore from Sodeshi-ga-ura, where offerings are made in the "Hitotsu-ishi {{ill|lt=Shinkōsai|Q123907414|qid=Q123907414|1=Shinko-shiki}}." === Yomotsu Hirasaka === A place identified as {{ill|lt=Yomotsu Hirasaka|Q22084535|qid=Q22084535|1=Yomotsu Hirasaka}} (boundary with {{ill|lt=Yomi|Q15846115|qid=Q15846115|1=Zbigniew Wilczkiewicz}} / {{ill|lt=Ne no Kuni|Q7555425|qid=Q7555425|1=Ne-no-kuni}}) appearing in Kiki myths such as {{ill|lt=Birth of the gods|Q3545159|qid=Q3545159|1=Kamiumi}} and {{ill|lt=Myths of Ōkuninushi|Q11433656|qid=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}} lies east of this shrine in Hiraga, Iya-chō, marked by a stone monument[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/admin/seisaku/koho/esque/2011/81/02.html Yomotsu Hirasaka, passage to the underworld] Shimane Prefecture "Shimanesque" No.81 (2011). === Ajisukitakahikone and Amenowakahiko === The place where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} is said to have toppled and kicked away {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amenowakahiko|1=UNKNOWN}}'s mourning hut is said to have been this shrine site.Manga artist {{ill|lt=Yasuhiko Yoshikazu|Q1049526|qid=Q1049526|1=Yoshikazu Yasuhiko}} places the presumed site here in his manga "Jinmu." === References === * Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki (eds.), 'New Popular Edition: Shrine Dictionary,' 1997 {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|qid=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, 'Annotated Study of Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,' 2006 {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|qid=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takine, 'Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo,' 2001 {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|qid=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 * {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), 'Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in' (new reprint), 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 ===Notes=== === External link === [https://iya-jinja.jp/ Iya Shrine] - official site {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]]
